provisions.title: Tittle
provisions.content: These By-laws may be cited as the Tabora Municipal Council ( Traffic ) By-laws, 2002.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these By-Laws:— " Carriage " means wagon, cart, trailer, rickshaw, bicycle, tricycle and every description of wheeled vehicle except a motor vehicle, and such appliances as baby carriages, wheelbarrows or other appliances. " Carriageway " means that portion of a street not reserved for pedes­trian traffic. " Commercial vehicle " means any vehicle constructed or adapted for use and used for the conveyance of goods in the course of trade or agriculture or for the conveyance in the course of their employment of persons who are in the employment of the owner of the vehicle. " Council " means the Tabora Municipal Council. " Crossing " means a crossing place for foot passengers indicated by a traffic sign. " Director " means the person for the time being holding the position or acting in the capacity of the Municipal Director. " Engineer " means the person for the time being holding the position or acting in the capacity of Engineer and includes his deputy or represen­tative. " Motor Cycle " means a motor vehicle designed to travel on not more than three wheels and having a tare weight not exceeding two hun­dred and fifty kilograms " Motor Omnibus " means any public service vehicle, which carries or is intended to carry passengers and their personal goods and is licensed to carry more than seven persons excluding the driver. " Motor Vehicle " includes a motor cycle and every description of ve­hicle propelled by means of mechanism contained within itself other than a vehicle used on specially prepared ways such as railways and tramways. " Park " means to draw up a vehicle and to leave such vehicle stationary (whether the driver remains in it or not) in a street or other public place. " Parking attendant " means any person as may be employed by the Council to serve as an attendant at parking place. " Pavement " means any impervious surface made of bitumen, concrete or concrete slab. " Pedestrian Crossing " means a part of a road marked with lines signals, signpost, signs or other device lawfully erected where vehicles must stop to allow pedestrians to cross. " Private hire Vehicle " means any vehicle, which is constructed or adapted solely or mainly, for the carriage of the passengers and their personal luggage other than taxicab but does not include a motorcycle. " Reserved Parking Place " means any area indicated as such by a traffic sign. " Street " includes any highway, road sanitary lane, thoroughfare or pub­lic place to which the public have access and includes bridge over which a roadway passes. " Tare Weight " means the weight of a vehicle unladen inclusive of the body and all parts (the heavier being taken where alternative bodies or part are used) which are necessary to or ordinarily used with the ve­hicle when used on a street. " Taxi Cab " means any public service vehicle, whether fitted with a taximeter or not, which carries or is intended to carry only passengers and their personal luggage and is licensed to carry not more than seven passengers excluding the driver. " Taxi Rank " means any area demarcated by the Council and indicated by notice board or signpost to be a place where taxicabs may stand for hire. " Traffic " means Vehicle moving along a road or street. " Traffic Island " means a raised area in the middle of a road driving two streams of traffic. " Traffic Sign " includes all signals, warning signposts, direction sign­posts signs, white lines or islands, or other devices lawfully erected, painted or placed on or near any highway for the guidance or direc­tion of any person using the highway. " Vehicle " includes motor vehicle and carriage.

provisions.title: Speed limits
provisions.content: (1) No person shall derive, pull of propel any vehicle in any street at a speed exceeding 50 Kilometers an hour or as indicated on the traffic sign lawfully erected or placed on such street (2) No person in any street or parking place shall repair, take down, assemble, wash, polish, clean, load or unload any vehicle, or any part thereof, in such a manner as to cause an obstruction or a nuisance except in an emergency and in such cases the vehicle shall be removed with the least possible delay. (3) No person shall park any disabled vehicle on any street or park­ing place, so as to cause any obstruction except in an emergency and in such a case the vehicle shall be removed with the least possible delay.

provisions.title: Parking on street corners and bus stops prohibited
provisions.content: No person shall park any vehicle within a distance of ten metres from any corner or within a distance of fifteen metres from either side of a place at which a traffic sign has been lawfully erected or placed indicating that such place is a stopping place for motor omnibuses, or in any position which is within a distance of ten metres from a traffic island.

provisions.title: Parking prohibited
provisions.content: No person shall park any vehicle in any street where a traffic sign indicating that parking is prohibited has been lawfully erected or placed.

provisions.title: Parking on footpaths
provisions.content: No person shall cause or permit any vehicle to be parked on any footpath or on any part of a street reserved for pedestrian traffic.

provisions.title: Parking spaces not to be used for selling goods
provisions.content: No person shall use, or cause to be used, any vehicle standing or any parking place for the purpose of effecting any sale, purchase or ex­change of goods of any kind.

provisions.title: Refuse on parking places
provisions.content: No person shall deposit, or cause to be deposited, on any parking place any refuse or garbage otherwise than in receptacles provided for the reception of such refuse or garbage.

provisions.title: One-way streets
provisions.content: No person shall drive, pull or propel any vehicle, or ride any cycle, or ride or drive any animal in any street in which the Council has erected or places a traffic sign indicating one way traffic, except in such direction as is indicated by such traffic sign. The streets in which traffic may travel in one direction only are specified in the Second Schedule to these By-laws.

provisions.title: Omnibuses plying from
provisions.content: Every motor omnibus and every public service vehicle engaged in carrying passengers (except a Taxi cab and Private hire Vehicle ) plying to arid from the Council shall arrive at and depart from an omni­bus stand duly appointed as such by the Council under section 26 of the Traffic Ordinance. No person shall alight from or enter any such motor omnibus or public service Vehicle except at an omnibus stand; similarly no goods shall be loaded on to or offloaded from such public service vehicle except at a duly appointed bus stand. The stand, which has been so appointed by the Council , is described in the Third Schedule to these By-laws.

provisions.title: Omnibuses operating an internal bus service within the Municipality bus stops
provisions.content: No person shall stop any motor omnibus duly appointed to oper­ate an internal omnibus service within the Council for the purpose of setting down a passenger or passengers or for permitting a passenger or passengers to enter such motor omnibus at any place other than at a place indicated by a sign lawfully erected or placed as a motor omnibus stopping place.

No person shall stop any motor omnibus for the purpose of setting down or picking up any passengers, parcels, packages or goods or any description other than at a place indicated by a traffic sign law­fully erected or placed as a motor omnibus stopping place.

provisions.title: Keep left at roundabouts
provisions.content: At all places where traffic islands have been or shall be installed within the Council all vehicles or animals approaching such islands shall keep to the left thereof and proceed round the island in a clock­wise direction. First Schedule (By-law No. 2) Fees for use or reserved parking place A. Reserved Parking place (from Monday to Friday 8.00 am to 4.00 pm) TShs. 200/= per Hr. Second Schedule (By-law No. 9) Streets in which traffic may travel in one direction only: Description of: Direction in which traffic may travel: 1. Balewa, between its junctions with Market Street and Gongoni Street. From Market Street to Gongoni Street. 2. Usagara From Ujiji Street to Manyema Street. Third Schedule (By-law No. 11) The areas bounded by Usagara Street, Ujiji Street and the Caltex Service Station. Fourth Schedule (By-law No. 17) Taxi Ranks The area on the south side of Ujiji Street immediately opposite to the Bus Stand. The taxi rank immediately in front of Tabora Station. The area in front of Maua Guest House junction of School/Itetemia. The area in front of Fourways Bar junction between Jamhuri Street and Nyamwezi street. The area in front of Kitete Hospital - Kitete road. The area infront of Isevya market. The area opposite Garden bar Boma Road. The area adjacent Railway Station Fifth Schedule (By-laws No. 21) Traffic Signs 1. Bollards 2. Carriageway markings 3. Children (School). 4. Danger Signs, Dimensions and Colours. 5. Dangerous cross roads. 6. Dangerous Junction for Pedestrian Crossing. 7. Dangerous Junction to Right. 8. Dangerous Junction to Right 9. Gate or Level Crossing Barrier. 10. General 11. Halt. 12. Hospital 13. Keep Left. 14. Level Crossing Warning Cross 15. No Right (or Left) Turn Prohibition Sign. 16. Narrow Bridge. 17. No Waiting or Parking. 18. Pedestrian. 19. Restriction Notice. 20. Road Narrows Dangerously. 21. Standard Parking Signs. 22. Speed limit. 23. Speed limit De-restriction Signs. 24. Slow. 25. Street Parking Signs 26. Traffic Circles. 27. Total Prohibition. 28. Turn Left. 29.
